pieces.hook: 'RAG is dead' — wrong take, wrong reason
pieces.angle: Defend RAG as infrastructure, not hype. Builders who dismiss it never served real queries at scale.

pieces.hook: Agentic RAG is not smarter RAG. It's a different architecture.
pieces.angle: Classic RAG is a pipeline. Agentic RAG is a control loop. Break down the architectural difference.

pieces.hook: Agents need vector search more than they need RAG
pieces.angle: Retrieval quality > generation quality for agentic systems. What changed on AI Employee.

pieces.hook: RAG for Indian SMBs: the gap nobody is filling
pieces.angle: What breaks in generic RAG stacks for Indian SMB contexts — Hinglish queries, scanned PDFs, regional spelling.

pieces.hook: The RAG security problem your deployment doesn't account for
pieces.angle: Adversarial hubness attacks on RAG — poisoned vectors that always get retrieved. How to defend.

pieces.hook: Why 90% of RAG demos fail in production (it's not the model)
pieces.angle: Retrieval quality, chunk strategy, evaluation. Most builders skip eval entirely.

pieces.hook: Stop building RAG prototypes. Start building RAG systems.
pieces.angle: The evaluation gap between 80% demo accuracy and 99% production accuracy. How to build an eval loop first.

pieces.hook: The chunking decision that broke my RAG system (and the fix)
pieces.angle: Fixed vs semantic vs hierarchical chunking. When each breaks. What AI Employee's catalogue queries needed.

pieces.hook: Why I didn't use Mem0 for AI Employee's memory layer
pieces.angle: Vertical memory beats horizontal. Mem0 is a building block, not a solution for Indian SMB customer service.

pieces.hook: 3 RAG failures specific to Indian SMB contexts
pieces.angle: Hinglish query breakdowns. Scanned PDF catalogues losing metadata. Regional spelling variation + embeddings.
